Mallardo clan.description: The Mallardo clan is a Camorra clan based in Giugliano, Campania, Italy, founded by Francesco Mallardo in the late 1970s. It is a key member of the Secondigliano Alliance, considered the most powerful active Camorra group. The clan is involved in racketeering, smuggling, extortion, and drug trafficking, and has been targeted by Italian authorities with asset seizures and arrests.

Finks Motorcycle Club.description: Australian outlaw motorcycle club formed in Sydney in 1969. The club has chapters in multiple Australian states and internationally. Known for its logo based on the jester Bung from The Wizard of Id cartoon. The club has been involved in criminal activities and conflicts with other bikie groups, and faced government restrictions in South Australia.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Finks as rivals in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros , Finks , Gypsy Jokers , Hells Angels , Mongols , Notorious , Rebels and Red Devils

Sabaot Land Defence Force.description: The Sabaot Land Defence Force (SLDF) was a guerrilla militia operating in the Mount Elgon District of Kenya from 2005 to 2008. It drew members from the Sabaot people and was accused of killing over 600 people, committing atrocities including murder, torture, and rape, and displacing more than 66,000 people. The group was funded by local taxation and had a parallel administration system. It was led by Wycliffe Matakwei Komon and was dismantled by 2008.

Original Red Devils Motorcycle Club.description: Canadian outlaw motorcycle club based in Southern Ontario, founded in Hamilton in 1948. It operated three chapters in Ontario and was known for staying small and maintaining respect among other clubs. The club patched over to Bacchus Motorcycle Club in 2014. It was listed as an Outlaw Motorcycle Gang by Criminal Intelligence Service Canada.

descriptions: Original Red Devils Motorcycle Club was absorbed by Bacchus through a patch-over.
edges.evidence_quote: On 8 November 2014, Bacchus MC patched over their closest ally, the Original Red Devils Motorcycle Club, to create the first Ontario expansion of the club absorbing the Red Devils three Ontario chapters.

descriptions: Red Devils are listed as an allied smaller motorcycle gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels are allied with numerous smaller motorcycle gangs – such as the ... Red Devils

descriptions: Hells Angels formed alliances with Red Devils.
edges.evidence_quote: Additionally, the club has also formed alliances with various street gangs, including the Independent Soldiers, the Red Scorpions, the United Nations and the White Boy Posse, and smaller motorcycle gangs, such as Bacchus, the Gate Keepers and the Red Devils.

descriptions: The two clubs share a brotherhood patch indicating a lasting bond.
edges.evidence_quote: All Bacchus MC and Original Red Devils MC members also sport a brotherhood patch depicting the lasting 1% bond between the two clubs.

descriptions: The Original Red Devils MC merged into Bacchus MC in late 2014.
edges.evidence_quote: They would end up patching over to long-time ally Bacchus Motorcycle Club in late 2014, with their three chapters Hamilton , Chatham-Kent , and Sudbury joining Bacchus MC.

descriptions: Original Red Devils MC merged into Bacchus in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: This club patched over to the East Coast based Bacchus in 2015.

descriptions: The Original Red Devils have good relationships with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: All of this has led to the Red Devils being "comfortable in their own skin," and have good relationships with other clubs, including the Hells Angels.

Hawkhurst Gang.description: The Hawkhurst Gang was a notorious criminal organisation involved in smuggling throughout south-east England from 1735 until 1749. Based in Hawkhurst, Kent, they extended their influence along the South coast and raided the Custom House in Poole. After a defeat by the Goudhurst militia in 1747, leaders Arthur Gray and Thomas Kingsmill were executed in 1748 and 1749, respectively.

Ringvereine.description: Criminal gangs operating in late 19th- and early 20th-century Germany, notably the Weimar period. Formed in the 1890s as convicts associations to aid reintegration, they became fronts for illegal activities, practicing witness intimidation and providing alibis. Banned by the Nazis in 1933 but persisted despite efforts to suppress them.

descriptions: Ringvereine is the union that emerged when clubs joined Ring Berlin.
edges.evidence_quote: The term emerged for a union with criminal orientation when several clubs by former prisoners joined the holding organization "Ring Berlin".

descriptions: Sin City Deciples is reportedly allied with Mixbreed MC.
edges.evidence_quote: The club is reportedly allied with the Dahuru MC and Mixbreed MC.

descriptions: La Mirada Punks is subservient to Nazi Lowriders.
edges.evidence_quote: Many of these Peckerwood gangs in California are subservient to the authority of Nazi Lowriders, which includes the punk gangs Vicious Circle and La Mirada Punks, as well as Insane White Boys, Independent Skins, Orange County Skins, and a rigid alliance with Southern California Skinhead Alliance (SoCal Skins).

Black Jackets.description: Primarily ethnically Turkish criminal gang active in Germany, the Netherlands, and Denmark. Formed in 1985 in Heidenheim an der Brenz by immigrants from Turkey, Italy, and former Yugoslavia. Engages in arms trafficking, drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, prostitution, burglary, and vandalism. Claims to be a brotherhood rather than an outlaw motorcycle club or criminal organization.

Forty-Two Gang.description: The Forty-Two Gang was a teenage street gang in Chicago active from 1925 to 1934. It served as a farm team for future members of the Chicago Outfit, producing notable mobsters like Sam Giancana. The gang engaged in vandalism, theft, burglary, and armed robbery, and was known for its violence. Many members were killed, wounded, or imprisoned.
